# On frames vs judgement

A fictional essayist writes, repeatedly across their catalog:

"The frame you pick determines the answer. Most disagreements are frame
disputes disguised as fact disputes. If two smart people are arguing
past each other, they are using different frames."

This shows up in three of their essays. Another recurring move:

"When a decision feels obvious, that is a flag. Obvious answers get less
scrutiny. The times I have been most wrong are the times I felt most sure."

Debate: speed vs quality — the essayist argues for quality but only if you
can define the specific quality axis that matters. Otherwise ship and learn.
